name: Generate message
README
info:
# Humidity Sensor Notification

## Summary

This flow will send an email notificaton if any `waterLeakDetector` devices in a WIoTP organization report a relative humidity value over the configured max threshold.

## Setup

1. Configure an API Key for the `ibm iot in` node:

- Add API Key
- Add API Token
- Set the server messaging address (`{orgId}.messaging.internetofthings.ibmcloud.com`)
- Set a unique application ID

2. Configure smtp authentication for the `mail out` node:

- Set smtp server
- Set smtp port
- Set smtp user ID
- Set smtp password
- 

3. Set the humidity threshold in the `switch` node

## Input

The `ibm iot in` node will subscribe to `status` events for a `waterLeakDetector` device in a Watson IoT Platform organization. 

The flow expects the `status` event payload to contain `temperature` and `humidity` fields:

```
{
    "temperature": 24.24,
    "humidity": 2.01
}
```

The temperature value unit is degrees celcius. The humidity value unit is relative humidity. Both values are float types.

name: README
info:
# Humidity Sensor View

## Summary

This flow will generate a visualization with historical and real-time dashboards of an environmental sensor.

The visualization can be viewed at `{urlOfNodeRedInstance}/ui`.

## Setup

1. Configure an API Key for the `ibm iot in` node:

- Add API Key
- Add API Token
- Set the server messaging address (`{orgId}.messaging.internetofthings.ibmcloud.com`)
- Set a unique application ID

## Input

The `ibm iot in` node will subscribe to `status` events for a `waterLeakDetector` device in a Watson IoT Platform organization. 

The flow expects the `status` event payload to contain `temperature` and `humidity` fields:

```
{
    "temperature": 24.24,
    "humidity": 2.01
}
```

The temperature value unit is degrees celcius. The humidity value unit is relative humidity. Both values are float types.

README
info:
# Humidity Sensor Simulation

## Summary

This flow will provision and simulate a configurable amount of devices outputting environmental sensor data to WIoTP.

## Setup

1. Configure an API Key for the `device-type-manager` node:

- Add API Key
- Add API Token

2. Configure an API Key for the `device-manager` node:

- Add API Key
- Add API Token

3. Configure an API Key for the `ibm iot out` node:

- Add API Key
- Add API Token
- Set the server messaging address (`{orgId}.messaging.internetofthings.ibmcloud.com`)
- Set a unique application ID

4. Trigger the `Provision` injection node to provision the device type and devices.

5. Enable and a repeat interval time in the `Simulate sensor devices` node to simulate environmental sensor data.

## Configuration

The `Persist config` change node sets a few properties that can be retrieved from the flow's context:

`numOfDevices` sets the number of devices to provision and simulate. Default is `10`.

`deviceTypeId` is the device type ID to use for the simulated devices. Default is `waterLeakDetector`.

`deviceIdPrefix` is the prefix of the device ID for the simulated devices. Default is `20West` (for the 20th IoT HQ floor).

`eventId` is the event ID the simulated devices will be publishing to. Default is `status`.

## Output

The `ibm iot out` node will publish `status` events for the `waterLeakDetector` devices to a WIoTP organization. 

The `status` event payload will contain `temperature` and `humidity` fields:

```
{
    "temperature": 24.24,
    "humidity": 2.01
}
```

The temperature value unit is degrees celcius. The humidity value unit is relative humidity. Both values are float types.

name: CSS Style Sheet
format:
<link href="https://fonts.googleapis.com/css?family=IBM+Plex+Sans" rel="stylesheet"/>

<style>
    :root{
        --color: 0, 255, 0;
        --prevColor: 0, 255, 0;
    }
    body.nr-dashboard-theme {
        font-family: 'IBM Plex Sans Condensed', -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, Segoe UI, Roboto, Oxygen-Sans, Ubuntu, Cantarell, Helvetica Neue, sans-serif !important;
        font-weight:bold;
    }
    .map{
        background-image: url('http://ricoh-data-visualization.eu-de.mybluemix.net/img/watersensor-floorplan-20.png');
        background-size: contain;
        background-repeat: no-repeat;
        width: 100%;
        height: 300px;
    }
    .sensor{
        height: 10px;
        width: 10px;
        position: absolute;
        border-radius: 50%;
        background-size: cover;
        color:rgba(var(--color), 1);
        background-color: rgba(var(--color), 1);
        visibility: hidden;
    }
    .popup{
        height: 15px;
        width: 30px;
        top:-20px;
        right:-20px;
        position: relative;
        border-radius: 20%;
        background-size: cover;
        background-color: rgba(var(--color), 0.5);
        text-align: center;
        font-size: 10px;
        color: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.75);
        z-index:10;
    }
    .sensorId{
        left:-40px;
        bottom:-10px;
        position: relative;
        width: 80px;
        color: rgba(154, 162, 172, 0.5);
        font-size: 10px;
        text-align: center;
        z-index: 2;
        visibility: visible;
    }
    #s20WestSensor1{
        top: 14%;
        left: 25%;
    }
    #s20WestSensor2{
        top: 8%;
        left: 34%;
    }
    #s20WestSensor3{
        top: 6%;
        left: 50%;
    }
    #s20WestSensor4{
        top: 8%;
        left: 74%;
    }
    #s20WestSensor5{
        top: 14%;
        left: 84.5%;
    }
    #s20EastSensor1{
        bottom: 15%;
        left: 9%;
    }
    #s20EastSensor2{
        bottom: 10%;
        left: 20%;
    }
    #s20EastSensor3{
        bottom: 10%;
        left: 58%;
    }
    #s20EastSensor4{
        bottom: 17.5%;
        left: 67%;
    }
    .pinging{
        animation: pingingEffect 4s infinite;
    }
    .animated{
        animation: pingingEffect 5s, changeEffect 5s;
    }
    @keyframes changeEffect {
        0%  {background-color:rgba(var(--prevColor), 1);}
        100%{background-color:rgba(var(--color), 1);}
    }
    @keyframes pingingEffect{
        0%  {color:rgba(var(--color), 0.5); box-shadow:0 0 0 0;}
        100%{color:rgba(var(--color), 0); box-shadow:0 0 5px 25px;}
    }
</style>
